methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $169M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.8B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Helion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series F), post-money $5.4B

data.narrative:
CarbonCure Technologies, founded by Robert Niven, is a Series F climate and energy company that has raised $169M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private frontier-tech peers in the climate and energy sector, including Helion, TAE Technologies, Form Energy, and Watershed, alongside one public comparable (Lanzatech, LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using total capital raised as a proxy, applies a 1.3x multiplier typical of Series F-to-next-stage progression, yielding $220M. The peer-median method examines four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $1.8B (Watershed) to $6.0B (TAE Technologies), producing a median valuation of $4.5B. The peer sample includes Helion at $5.4B in Series F and Form Energy and TAE Technologies both in Series G.

The two methods produce a wide implied range: $187M (low) to $5.1B (high), with a median of $2.3B. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively near recent capital deployment, while the peer-median reflects valuations across a maturing frontier-tech cohort at more advanced stages. Key uncertainties include the relevance of the peer set given product maturity and commercial traction variance across climate subsectors, and whether CarbonCure's metrics more closely align with near-term or later-stage comparable outcomes. The range suggests fair value lies materially above cost basis but reflects material dispersion tied to execution risk and market positioning within the climate tech landscape.
